title: Flavorful Algerian Potato Tagine
description: A hearty and flavorful Algerian dish featuring yukon gold potatoes in a rich and aromatic tagine sauce, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This paleo-friendly recipe is kid-friendly and has a nutty and slightly shaggy texture.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay pot over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until it is translucent and starting to caramelize.
Add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly to prevent burning.
Add the ground cumin, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper, and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Add the cubed potatoes, chicken broth, and chopped parsley to the clay pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 20 minutes, or until the potatoes are tender and the liquid has been absorbed.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with additional chopped parsley if desired.
